form_semantics.justification: All inputs have labels, radio groups use nested fieldset/legend, toggles use checkbox inputs, file upload and textarea are properly labelled, and the form element is present. Minor gap: the two standalone privacy checkboxes ('Show online status', 'Allow search engines to index my profile') are grouped in a div but not wrapped in a fieldset with a legend describing the group.

element_choice.justification: Every interactive element uses the correct native HTML: button[type=button] for Delete Account, button[type=submit] for Save Changes, input[type=file] for avatar, input[type=checkbox] for toggles, input[type=radio] for radio groups, textarea for bio, and input[type=text] for display name. No div or span used in place of a semantic element.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one h1 ('Account Settings') on the page. Section headings are conveyed through fieldset legend elements rather than duplicate heading tags, which is appropriate per the rubric. No skipped heading levels.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and correctly: aria-label on the form and nav landmarks where native HTML needs a name, aria-describedby on bio and avatar inputs for hint text, aria-hidden on the decorative avatar preview div. No redundant roles on native elements, no aria compensating for non-semantic markup.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link present targeting main, header and main landmarks used correctly, nav has aria-label. tabindex=-1 on main supports skip-link focus. No unnecessary landmarks.

content_realism.justification: All labels and hint text are realistic and descriptive (e.g., 'Up to 500 characters. Visible on your public profile.', 'Accepted formats: JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP. Maximum 5 MB.'). No placeholder or lorem ipsum content.

expectations_met: Radio button groups are within a <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
Toggle switches use checkbox inputs (not custom div patterns)
File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label
'Delete Account' is a <button> — not a link
Legends serve as visual headings (no redundant heading + legend for same text)
Radio buttons have matching name attributes within their group
The form as a whole is a <form> element
Section headings use consistent heading levels
expectations_missed: The two privacy checkboxes ('Show online status', 'Allow search engines to index my profile') are not wrapped in a <fieldset> with a <legend> — they are related controls that benefit from grouping semantics, currently only grouped by a div
additional_observations: Good use of autocomplete='nickname' on the display name field
aria-hidden='true' correctly applied to decorative avatar preview div
tabindex='-1' on <main> correctly supports skip-link target focus
Nested fieldset pattern (Notification frequency inside Notifications, Profile visibility inside Privacy) is semantically correct and handles the radio sub-groups well
